name: Cheese Soup (Lanarkshire)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt a small amount of butter or margarine in a saucepan.
2. Add the finely chopped onion and fry gently until softened, without browning.
3. Stir in the hot water and bring to a gentle simmer with the onion.
4. Gradually add the milk, stirring continuously until the mixture reaches approximately one quart (1 liter) in volume.
5. Mix the flour with an equal amount of milk to create a smooth paste, then stir this into the saucepan, ensuring it is well incorporated.
6. Season the soup with salt and pepper as desired.
7. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
8. Reduce heat to low and let simmer until the soup thickens, about 10-15 minutes.
9. Just before serving, stir in the finely grated Stilton cheese until melted and smooth.
Warning: Ensure all steps are completed carefully to avoid accidents or burns from hot liquids.
